title: Lab 01: Networking and Hostname
description: Configure persistent client networking and a matching server hostname for RHCSA practice

tasks:
On client, configure the active client connection with the following persistent settings:

IP Address: 192.168.122.25
Netmask: 255.255.255.0
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client.netlab.local
On server, set the persistent hostname to repo.netlab.local.

On client, add a persistent hosts entry with the following value:

Hostname: repo.netlab.local
Address: 192.168.122.3
hints: Use nmcli or nmtui for the client network profile.
Use hostnamectl for the persistent server hostname.
Use /etc/hosts for the client static host entry.
checks: hostnamectl --static | grep -qx 'client.netlab.local' && nmcli connection show "System eth1" >/dev/null && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.25/24' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "System eth1")" = '192.168.122.3' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "System eth1")" = 'manual' && test "$(nmcli -g connection.autoconnect connection show "System eth1")" = 'yes'
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.25/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
